Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded image or document by a user, storing its metadata and extracted text.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileUrl.description: URL where the original document file is stored, if applicable.

uploadDateTime.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

documentType.description: Categorization of the document content (e.g., identity proof, billing, medical report, general).

rawExtractedText.description: The raw text extracted from the document using OCR or initial AI processing.

editedExtractedText.description: The text after users have made corrections or edits to the raw extracted text.

status.description: Current processing status of the document (e.g., UPLOADED, PROCESSING, TEXT_EXTRACTED, DATA_EXTRACTED, COMPLETED).

ExtractedIdentityDetails.title: ExtractedIdentityDetails
ExtractedIdentityDetails.description: Stores structured personal details extracted from identity proof documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractedIdentityDetails entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which these details were extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedIdentityDetails)

name.description: Full name of the person as extracted from the identity document.

dateOfBirth.description: Date of birth extracted from the identity document.

address.description: Residential address extracted from the identity document.

idNumber.description: Unique identification number (e.g., passport number, driver's license number) from the document.

issueDate.description: Date when the identity document was issued.

expiryDate.description: Date when the identity document expires.

documentTypeIdentified.description: Specific type of identity document identified (e.g., 'DriverLicense', 'Passport', 'NationalID').

ExtractedBillingDetails.title: ExtractedBillingDetails
ExtractedBillingDetails.description: Stores structured details extracted from billing documents like invoices or receipts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractedBillingDetails entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which these details were extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedBillingDetails)

vendorName.description: Name of the vendor or service provider on the bill.

invoiceDate.description: Date of the invoice or bill.

dueDate.description: Payment due date specified on the bill.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the bill.

currency.description: The currency of the total amount (e.g., USD, EUR).

lineItemIds.description: References to the individual LineItem entities associated with this bill. (Relationship: ExtractedBillingDetails 1:N LineItem)

LineItem.title: LineItem
LineItem.description: Represents a single line item within a billing document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LineItem entity.

billingDetailsId.description: Reference to the ExtractedBillingDetails this line item belongs to. (Relationship: ExtractedBillingDetails 1:N LineItem)

description.description: Description of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item.

lineTotal.description: The total amount for this line item (quantity * unitPrice).

ExtractedMedicalDetails.title: ExtractedMedicalDetails
ExtractedMedicalDetails.description: Stores structured diagnostic and medical details extracted from hospital or medical reports.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractedMedicalDetails entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which these details were extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedMedicalDetails)

patientName.description: Name of the patient mentioned in the medical report.

dateOfReport.description: Date when the medical report was issued.

diagnosis.description: List of diagnoses or medical conditions identified in the report.

diseasesIdentified.description: List of specific diseases or conditions mentioned.

medications.description: List of medications prescribed or mentioned in the report.

doctorName.description: Name of the doctor who issued or signed the report.

hospitalName.description: Name of the hospital or clinic where the report originated.

findings.description: General findings or summary from the medical report.

definition.description: Stores metadata and extracted text for a user's uploaded document. The `userId` field within the document is redundant with the path but ensures authorization independence for security rules, allowing direct comparison with `request.auth.uid`. The `status` field models the document's processing state.

Stores structured identity details extracted from a specific document. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document from which details were extracted.

params.name: detailsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extracted identity details document.

definition.description: Stores structured billing details extracted from a specific document. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document from which details were extracted.

params.name: detailsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extracted billing details document.

definition.description: Stores individual line items belonging to a specific set of extracted billing details. Includes denormalized 'userId', 'documentId', and 'billingDetailsId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the document related to these billing details.

params.name: billingDetailsId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent extracted billing details document.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the individual line item document.

definition.description: Stores structured medical details extracted from a specific document. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'documentId' fields for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document from which details were extracted.

params.name: detailsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extracted medical details document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes strict user data isolation and simplified security rules by leveraging hierarchical paths and aggressive denormalization of authorization context. All user-uploaded documents and their associated extracted details are nested under a user-specific path: `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}/...`. This design directly enforces path-based ownership, making it trivially simple to write security rules that grant access only if `request.auth.uid == userId` in the path.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To achieve Authorization Independence and eliminate the need for costly and fragile `get()` calls in security rules, crucial authorization fields (`userId`, `documentId`, and `billingDetailsId` where applicable) are denormalized and stored directly within every document in subcollections. For example, `ExtractedIdentityDetails` documents, although nested under a specific `documentId` and `userId` in the path, will explicitly contain `userId` and `documentId` fields within their data. This allows security rules to evaluate ownership solely based on `request.auth.uid` matching `resource.data.userId` without traversing parent documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure inherently supports secure `list` operations (Query Against Paths) without requiring complex filtering logic in rules. Because all user-specific data is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}/`, any `list` query must necessarily begin by specifying the `userId` in the path (e.g., `db.collection('users').doc(myUserId).collection('documents').get()`). The security rules can then simply verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, and that the `resource.data.userId` within each document also matches, guaranteeing that only the authenticated user's data is returned. This prevents users from accidentally or maliciously querying across other users' data.

**Structural Segregation & Explicit State Modeling:** The `Document` entity's `status` field (e.g., `UPLOADED`, `PROCESSING`, `COMPLETED`) is used to manage the lifecycle of a document, effectively replacing the need for a separate, problematic global `pending_verifications` collection. This adheres to Structural Segregation by keeping all status-related information within the user's private document collection, simplifying queries and security. Any 'pending' documents a user might need to view can be queried by `status` from their own `/users/{userId}/documents` collection. This directly addresses the `FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions: ... path: "/databases/(default)/documents/pending_verifications"` by eliminating the global `pending_verifications` collection, which would inherently violate secure QAPs for private user data if it contained documents from multiple users.
